raw_consumer_statement:
I booked a room with an ocean view through XXXX for XXXX XXXX XXXX  at the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX , CA. When we got there, the hotel did a bait and switch and told us that for the first night we would not have an ocean view and would move us the next day. I insisted that we get what we paid for in advance. The front desk person, XXXX, tried to accommodate my request, but she was over ruled by her manager.

We checked in, went and got something to eat, but I felt ill after coming back to the hotel. I went to the front desk and cancelled my last two nights. XXXX said that the last two nights were cancelled and might take two days to appear on our statement. After waiting the two days the credit on my Macy 's XXXX XXXX didn't show up. I called the XXXX XXXX back and they told me that since I booked thru XXXXXXXX XXXX that they were the ones who were suppose to credit by account. When I contacted XXXX, they told me that it was up to the hotel to give me the credit. I also contacted Macys and they said a credit would be forth coming, but it might take a statement for it to appear. It's been two cycles and the credit still has not appeared. I paid on my credit card for the one night for {$250.00}, plus tax. The remaining bill Macy 's is still trying to get me to pay is {$560.00}. I shouldn't have to pay since I cancelled the last two nights due to a medical emergency. Plus XXXX, the front desk clerk told me that it was taken care of. Both the XXXX XXXX XXXX, and XXXX com are passing the buck. And Macy 's is trying to stick me with paying for two night stays that I had cancelled.

Reservation # to the XXXX XXXX XXXX is XXXX XXXX address is : XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX address is : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX. Their phone number is XXXX Macy 's XXXX XXXX XXXX ( CitiBank ) address is : Citibank N.A., XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX
